title: exsertanthera longa
intro: Exsertanthera longa, also known as the Indian long pepper, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayan region and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding digestion. Exsertanthera longa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and infusions, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various ailments.
